questions.question: Segment tree supports:
options: O(log n) range queries and O(log n) point updates
questions.answer: O(log n) range queries and O(log n) point updates

questions.question: Segment tree space requirement:
questions.answer: O(4n) — typically 4*n array

questions.question: Fenwick Tree (BIT) supports:
options: Prefix sum queries and point updates in O(log n), simpler than segment tree
questions.answer: Prefix sum queries and point updates in O(log n), simpler than segment tree

questions.question: Trie is best for:
options: Prefix search — O(m) insert/query by key length m
questions.answer: Prefix search — O(m) insert/query by key length m

questions.question: Union-Find (DSU) with path compression + rank:
options: O(α(n)) amortized — near O(1) — inverse Ackermann
questions.answer: O(α(n)) amortized — near O(1) — inverse Ackermann

questions.question: Sparse Table for Range Minimum Query (RMQ):
options: O(n log n) build, O(1) query — overlapping intervals trick
questions.answer: O(n log n) build, O(1) query — overlapping intervals trick

questions.question: When is a Fenwick Tree preferred over Segment Tree?
options: Simple prefix sums with point updates — smaller constant, simpler code
questions.answer: Simple prefix sums with point updates — smaller constant, simpler code

questions.question: Square root decomposition solves range queries in:
options: O(sqrt(n)) per query — simpler to implement than segment tree
questions.answer: O(sqrt(n)) per query — simpler to implement than segment tree

questions.question: A balanced BST (e.g., AVL) provides:
options: O(log n) insert, delete, search — height guaranteed O(log n)
questions.answer: O(log n) insert, delete, search — height guaranteed O(log n)

questions.question: LRU Cache is implemented using:
options: Hash map + doubly linked list — O(1) get/put
questions.answer: Hash map + doubly linked list — O(1) get/put

sublevels.theory:
**Lazy propagation in Segment Tree:** defer range updates — O(log n) range updates.

**2D Segment Tree / BIT:** for 2D range queries — O(log^2 n) per operation.

**Persistent Segment Tree:** keeps all historical versions — O(log n) update creates new path only.

**Order Statistics Tree (OST):** augmented BST — O(log n) rank/select operations.

**k-d tree:** space-partitioning for k-dimensional points — O(log n) average nearest neighbor.

questions.question: Lazy propagation in segment tree enables:
options: O(log n) range updates (e.g., add value to range) — defers work until needed
questions.answer: O(log n) range updates (e.g., add value to range) — defers work until needed

questions.question: Persistent segment tree allows:
options: Access to historical versions — each update creates O(log n) new nodes, previous versions preserved
questions.answer: Access to historical versions — each update creates O(log n) new nodes, previous versions preserved

questions.question: 2D BIT (Fenwick Tree) for 2D prefix sums — complexity per operation:
options: O(log n * log m) for an n×m grid
questions.answer: O(log n * log m) for an n×m grid

questions.question: Order Statistics Tree (OST) supports:
options: O(log n) rank (position of element) and select (k-th element)
questions.answer: O(log n) rank (position of element) and select (k-th element)

questions.question: k-d tree is used for:
options: Nearest neighbor search in k-dimensional space
questions.answer: Nearest neighbor search in k-dimensional space

questions.question: Merge sort tree enables:
options: Count elements in range [l,r] with value in [a,b] — O(log^2 n) per query
questions.answer: Count elements in range [l,r] with value in [a,b] — O(log^2 n) per query

questions.question: Wavelet tree enables:
options: O(log maxV) range kth minimum, quantile, and element count queries
questions.answer: O(log maxV) range kth minimum, quantile, and element count queries

questions.question: Link-Cut Tree supports:
options: Dynamic tree operations — link, cut, path aggregate queries in O(log n) amortized
questions.answer: Dynamic tree operations — link, cut, path aggregate queries in O(log n) amortized

questions.question: Fenwick Tree range update (add to range [l,r]):
options: Use two BITs or difference array technique — O(log n) update and query
questions.answer: Use two BITs or difference array technique — O(log n) update and query

questions.question: Offline query processing with segment tree (Mo's algorithm):
options: Order queries to minimize transitions — O((n+q)sqrt(n)) amortized
questions.answer: Order queries to minimize transitions — O((n+q)sqrt(n)) amortized

sublevels.theory:
**Splay tree:** self-adjusting BST — amortized O(log n), excellent cache behavior for non-uniform access.

**B+Tree:** leaf nodes linked in a doubly linked list for efficient range scans — used in databases.

**Fractal Cascading:** enables binary search across multiple sorted lists in O(log n + k).

**Cache-oblivious data structures:** optimal at all cache hierarchy levels without knowing cache parameters.

**Succinct data structures:** represent data in near-optimal bits — e.g., wavelet tree, FM-index for text.

questions.question: Splay tree's access lemma gives:
options: O(log n) amortized for all ops — recently accessed items near root
questions.answer: O(log n) amortized for all ops — recently accessed items near root

questions.question: B+ tree leaf nodes are linked because:
options: Range scans traverse leaves sequentially without climbing tree — O(range) for range queries
questions.answer: Range scans traverse leaves sequentially without climbing tree — O(range) for range queries

questions.question: Succinct data structure uses:
options: Near-optimal bits (within lower bound + o(n) — ops still O(1) or O(log n)
questions.answer: Near-optimal bits (within lower bound + o(n) — ops still O(1) or O(log n)

questions.question: FM-index enables:
options: Full-text search in O(n log n)
Compressed suffix array with O(m) pattern search and O(nH0) space (entropy-compressed)
questions.answer: Compressed suffix array with O(m) pattern search and O(nH0) space (entropy-compressed)

questions.question: van Emde Boas tree provides:
options: O(log log U) for insert, delete, successor — U = universe size
questions.answer: O(log log U) for insert, delete, successor — U = universe size

questions.question: X-fast trie vs Y-fast trie:
options: X-fast: O(log log U) query, O(U) space; Y-fast: O(log log U) query, O(n) space using hash tables
questions.answer: X-fast: O(log log U) query, O(U) space; Y-fast: O(log log U) query, O(n) space using hash tables

questions.question: Cache-oblivious algorithm benefit:
options: Optimal performance at all memory hierarchy levels without knowing cache parameters
questions.answer: Optimal performance at all memory hierarchy levels without knowing cache parameters

questions.question: Leftist tree enables:
options: O(log n) merge — leftist property: null-path length of left ≥ right
questions.answer: O(log n) merge — leftist property: null-path length of left ≥ right

questions.question: Kinetic data structure maintains:
options: Properties of moving points — handles events when ordering changes
questions.answer: Properties of moving points — handles events when ordering changes

questions.question: Half-open interval tree enables:
options: O(log n + k) stabbing and range queries for intervals
O(n log n) construction, O(1) query
questions.answer: O(log n + k) stabbing and range queries for intervals
